title: roasted american sweet potatoes casserole
description: a comforting american dish featuring roasted sweet potatoes, gooey marshmallows, and a hint of rum, perfect for a cozy winter evening. this recipe is egg-free-friendly and can be served as a side or a main course.

ingredients.name: sofritto
ingredients.notes: a mixture of sautéed carrots, celery, and onion

ingredients.name: marshmallows
ingredients.notes: mini marshmallows are best for even toasting

instructions: peel and dice the sweet potatoes into 1-inch cubes, place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, and roast for 20-25 minutes, or until tender and lightly browned.
in a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat, add the sofritto and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender and lightly browned.
stir in the light rum and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, or until the liquid has almost completely evaporated.
in a separate saucepan, melt the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat, add the heavy cream and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the mixture starts to simmer.
stir in the brown sugar, flour, salt, and cinnamon, and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the mixture thickens.
add the marshmallows to the saucepan and stir until they are well coated with the sweet potato mixture.

transfer the sweet potatoes to a 9x13-inch baking dish, top with the marshmallow mixture, and bake for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the marshmallows are golden brown and the casserole is bubbly.
remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for 10-15 minutes before serving.
serve warm, garnished with additional cinnamon and marshmallows if desired.
